text_excerpt: Tile Hill – £20m Pride in Place Funding - Taiwo Owatemi MP Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Invalid Path --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> External URL: https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/font-awesome/4.7.0/css/font-awesome.min.css --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Maybe Not Static File --> Excluded option --> Skip to content Homepage About Me Help & Advice My Work News & Updates Contact Me Office Policies Homepage About Me Help & Advice My Work News & Updates Contact Me Office Policies Search Search Tile hill £20m Pride in Place Funding Tile Hill is set to benefit from a major new long-term investment through the government’s Pride in Place programme. This means £2 million a year for the next 10 years — a total of £20 million invested directly into Tile Hill . This is a significant opportunity for local residents to shape how money is spent in their community. What is Pride in Place and what does it mean for Tile Hill? Pride in Place is a long-term programme focused on neighbourhoods that have been overlooked for too long. It is designed to: put power back into the hands of local people support stronger communities improve local places and opportunities Unlike traditional funding, decisions won’t be made in Whitehall – they will be shaped by local residents, community groups and organisations . Tile Hill has been identified as an area where long-term investment can make a real difference , with funding focused on a local neighbourhood and driven by community priorities The funding is deliberately flexible – so it reflects what local people actually want and need. This could include: improving high streets and public spaces investing in parks, green spaces and community facilities supporting young people and families tackling anti-social behaviour and improving safety bringing empty buildings back into use supporting local groups, services and opportunities The key principle is simple: local people decide the priorities. What it could fund Who decides How the money is spent? A local Neighbourhood Board will be set up to oversee the funding. This board will: include local residents and community voices work alongside the Council as the accountable body ensure decisions reflect the area — not just the “usual suspects” As the local MP, Taiwo will support the process and help ensure residents’ voices are heard — but this funding is designed to be led by the community . The next stage is public consultation . This is where local people shape what happens next — through sharing ideas, highlighting priorities, and helping guide how funding is used. This funding should reflect what matters to you. If you live in Tile Hill, Taiwo wants to hear your views on how this £20 million should be spent. Take the survey via the link below! Take the survey You can share: what you value most about Tile Hill what needs to improve your ideas for how this funding could make the biggest difference The more people take part, the stronger the case for delivering the right investment locally. Tile Hill deserves long-term investment and a real say over its future — this is our chance to make that happen together. Have your say Facebook Twitter Youtube Tiktok Linkedin Instagram let’s talk Book one of my surgery appointments If you want to book an appointment to discuss any issues that matter to you that I can help with, please complete the form below. Contact us This is the official website of Taiwo Owatemi, MP for Coventry North West. Please note that due to strict Parliamentary protocol I am only able to make enquiries on behalf of my own constituents.
